title: How much of China's crude demand will return?
section_path: How much of China's crude demand will return?
section_path_text: How much of China's crude demand will return?
summary:
Main
In this blog, we use a high-case scenario to assess plausible recovery in China's crude demand under current policy settings and market conditions. By Emma Li
With crude benchmarks retreating from above $100/b, market attention is shifting from supply disruptions to the post-crisis rebalancing of global oil markets, despite continued uncertainty over physical trade flows. Setting aside potential logistical disruptions, the global crude balance has already begun to shift.

children.title: Main
children.summary:
In this blog, we use a high-case scenario to assess plausible recovery in China's crude demand under current policy settings and market conditions. By Emma Li
With crude benchmarks retreating from above $100/b, market attention is shifting from supply disruptions to the post-crisis rebalancing of global oil markets, despite continued uncertainty over physical trade flows.

children.title: Near-term booster: fuel exports and state-led spending
section_path: Near-term booster: fuel exports and state-led spending
children.section_path_text: Near-term booster: fuel exports and state-led spending
children.summary: China removed its fuel export restrictions in early July after more than three months of severely constrained motor fuel exports. However, exports are unlikely to rebound immediately.

children.title: Mid-term catalyst: China's strategic and commercial crude stock expansion
section_path: Mid-term catalyst: China's strategic and commercial crude stock expansion
children.section_path_text: Mid-term catalyst: China's strategic and commercial crude stock expansion
children.summary: One of the key lessons from the Strait of Hormuz disruption is the strategic value of maintaining adequate crude inventories. India and several Southeast Asian countries had largely replenished their inventory losses by June, reflecting their relatively limited storage capacity and smaller absolute stock draws.

children.title: Long-term reality: structural demand erosion prevents a full recovery
section_path: Long-term reality: structural demand erosion prevents a full recovery
children.section_path_text: Long-term reality: structural demand erosion prevents a full recovery
children.summary: In July, China's State Council announced series of energy transition targets, including new energy vehicles (NEVs) to account for 30% of the vehicle fleet by 2030, up from around 12% at end-2025. However, the crisis has also accelerated structural changes in China's oil demand.
